danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat presence; real-time danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: distinct enemy sprite behavior and appearance in combat
danger_signals.meaning: hostile entity encountered; battle state active

danger_signals.visual_form: primitive dynamic lighting changes in dungeon view

safety_signals.visual_form: chamber with 24 mirrors containing frozen champions
safety_signals.meaning: safe starting zone; recruitment/party selection area

safety_signals.visual_form: gated or sealed first dungeon section
safety_signals.meaning: progression checkpoint; safe learning zone before deeper exploration

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: dungeon architecture controlled/inhabited by Grey Lord; firestaff location as faction stronghold

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: combat encounters as manifestations of chaos corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: deeper dungeon levels; final confrontation chamber

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: party composition shown through selectable frozen champions with fantasy archetypes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: party positioning in first-person view; character skill visualization through direct action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: frozen champions suspended in 24 mirrors; fantasy archetype character models visible as static portraits/forms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player selection mode; champions immobilized, awaiting resurrection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: first-person pseudo-3D dungeon view with real-time environmental rendering; characters visible in paper doll interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: party movement through dungeon corridors; real-time encounter triggering; skill improvement through direct action use

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enlarged first-person combat view with creature sprites; screamer enemies visible with distinct appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: real-time Active Time Battle system; creatures attack and respond; dynamic lighting shift; audio proximity cues trigger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: deeper dungeon sections; corruption visual markers; firestaff artifact visible or sought
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased enemy encounters; alternative ending branch if firestaff obtained but player exits dungeon without confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: artifact possession indicated in inventory/paper doll interface; visual confirmation of item acquisition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: branching narrative: can either confront Lord Chaos for true ending or leave dungeon for alternative ending

environmental_storytelling.example: first section is gated/contained, forcing player to complete setup before progression; deeper levels correlate with increased threat and lore significance
firestaff located in dungeon as central quest objective; its recovery directly enables confrontation with Lord Chaos, tying item significance to world-state change

24 frozen champions based on fantasy archetypes (Barbarian 'Halk', Druid 'Syra Child of Nature', Mage 'Alex Ander', Prophet 'Nabi') convey lore of prior adventurers sent into dungeon; their statuses (frozen/dead) show dungeon's lethal history
sound effects indicating creature proximity without visual confirmation; creates tension and danger perception through auditory cues rather than visual alone
primitive dynamic lighting changes suggest environmental hazards, enemy presence, or corrupted zones; light source manipulation communicates world threat level

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: engage in real-time combat; sprite animation and positioning indicate threat level; proximity triggers audio alert
suspended in mirrors; immobile; visual archetype presentation (barbarian musculature, natural druid elements) communicates role/class without explicit labels
final encounter manifestation; firestaff presence/absence determines if player can reach and defeat it; branching behavior based on world-state (player possession of firestaff)
skill improvement through direct action use; paper doll interface shows character status; first-person view reflects party health/capability state

notes: Dungeon Master (SNES 1993) communicates world-state primarily through: (1) Real-time audio proximity cues for danger sensing; (2) Paper doll interface showing party composition and status; (3) Dungeon architecture as gated progression system; (4) Artifact (firestaff) as state-change mechanic tied to narrative branches; (5) Champion archetype visuals providing implicit role/faction identity. The game lacks explicit faction color/sigil systems but uses artifact possession, dungeon depth, and encounter escalation to signal political/threat world-states. Dynamic lighting and sound design are primary danger manifestation methods rather than explicit visual warning systems.
